answer: Hello! 👋 Welcome to the NAUB Enquiry Chatbot. I'm your virtual assistant for Nigerian Army University Biu. How can I help you today? You can ask me about admissions, fees, programmes, hostels, academic calendar, and more!

questions: what kind of university is NAUB
answer: Nigerian Army University Biu (NAUB) is a federal university established in 2018 by the Nigerian Army. It is located in Biu, Borno State, Nigeria. NAUB was founded to provide quality tertiary education and is fully accredited by the National Universities Commission (NUC). The university is headed by the Vice-Chancellor and is under the supervision of the Nigerian Army Council. It offers undergraduate and postgraduate programmes across multiple faculties.

questions: how do I get to NAUB
answer: Nigerian Army University Biu (NAUB) is located in Biu, Borno State, Nigeria. The campus is situated along Maiduguri Road, Biu. Biu is a local government area in southern Borno State, accessible by road from Maiduguri (about 180km), Yola, and Gombe.

Here are the key NAUB contact details:

📞 **Main Switchboard:** +234-803-000-0000
🌐 **Website:** www.naub.edu.ng
📧 **General Email:** info@naub.edu.ng
📧 **Admissions:** admissions@naub.edu.ng
📧 **Bursary/Fees:** bursary@naub.edu.ng
📧 **ICT Unit:** ict@naub.edu.ng
📍 **Address:** NAUB Campus, Maiduguri Road, Biu, Borno State.

Office hours are Monday–Friday, 8:00 AM – 4:00 PM.

NAUB currently has the following Faculties:

1. 🔬 **Faculty of Science** — Offers BSc programmes in Computer Science, Mathematics, Physics, Chemistry, Biology, and Microbiology.
2. 📚 **Faculty of Humanities** — Includes English Language, History, Islamic Studies, and related disciplines.
3. 📊 **Faculty of Management Sciences** — Offers Accounting, Business Administration, and Economics.
4. 🏛️ **Faculty of Social Sciences** — Includes Mass Communication, Political Science, and Sociology.
5. ⚖️ **Faculty of Law** — LLB programme in Law.
6. 🎓 **Postgraduate School** — Masters and PhD programmes in select disciplines.

questions: what can I study at NAUB
answer:
NAUB offers the following undergraduate programmes:

**Faculty of Science:**
• B.Sc Computer Science
• B.Sc Mathematics
• B.Sc Physics
• B.Sc Chemistry
• B.Sc Biology
• B.Sc Microbiology

**Faculty of Humanities:**
• B.A English Language
• B.A History & Diplomatic Studies
• B.A Islamic Studies

**Faculty of Management Sciences:**
• B.Sc Accounting
• B.Sc Business Administration
• B.Sc Economics

**Faculty of Social Sciences:**
• B.Sc Mass Communication
• B.Sc Political Science
• B.Sc Sociology

**Faculty of Law:**
• LLB Law (5 years)

All programmes run for 4 years except Law (5 years). Postgraduate programmes are also available.

questions: what do I need for NAUB admission
how to get admission into naub
answer:
To gain admission into NAUB, you need to meet the following requirements:

**UTME (JAMB) Requirements:**
• Minimum JAMB score: 140 (general)
• Science-based courses: minimum 160
• Law: minimum 180
• NAUB JAMB Brochure Code: check www.naub.edu.ng

**O'Level Requirements:**
• Minimum of 5 credits in relevant subjects at WAEC/NECO/GCE
• Must include English Language and Mathematics (except for some Humanities programmes)
• Credits must be obtained in not more than 2 sittings

**Post-UTME Screening:**
• Purchase Post-UTME form on the NAUB portal (www.naub.edu.ng)
• Screening is conducted on campus
• Minimum JAMB score to qualify for screening: 140

**Direct Entry:**
• Holders of ND (Upper Credit), NCE, or A-Levels may apply via JAMB DE

Visit www.naub.edu.ng/admissions for the current admission form and deadline.

questions: when will admission list be out
how to check admission list naub
answer:
To check your NAUB admission status:

1. Visit **www.naub.edu.ng**
2. Click on **'Admissions'** then **'Check Admission Status'**
3. Enter your **JAMB Registration Number**
4. Your admission status will be displayed

Alternatively, check the **JAMB CAPS portal** at caps.jamb.gov.ng to see if you have been offered admission and to accept or reject the offer.

If you have further questions, contact the Admissions Office at **admissions@naub.edu.ng**.

questions: how much is fees at NAUB
answer:
NAUB school fees vary by faculty and student status. Below are approximate figures (verify at www.naub.edu.ng for current rates):

**Fresh Students (100 Level):**
• Science-based courses: ₦250,000 – ₦300,000 per session
• Arts/Social Science/Management: ₦200,000 – ₦250,000 per session
• Law: ₦300,000 – ₦350,000 per session

**Returning Students (200–400 Level):**
• Science-based: ₦200,000 – ₦250,000 per session
• Arts/Management/Social Science: ₦180,000 – ₦220,000 per session

**Acceptance Fee:** ₦50,000 (one-time, for newly admitted students)

**Payment Method:**
• Log in to the NAUB student portal at **portal.naub.edu.ng**
• Generate a Remita invoice
• Pay at any bank or via online banking

⚠️ Fees are subject to change. Always confirm on the official portal.

questions: how to get hostel at naub
answer:
NAUB provides on-campus hostel accommodation for both male and female students.

**Hostel Fees (per session):**
• Approximately ₦40,000 – ₦60,000 depending on room type

**How to Apply for Hostel:**
1. Log in to the NAUB student portal
2. Navigate to **'Accommodation'**
3. Apply during the hostel allocation window (usually at the start of each session)
4. Generate your Remita invoice and pay
5. Collect your bed space allocation from the Student Affairs Division

**Key Points:**
• Hostel spaces are limited and allocated on a first-come, first-served basis
• Priority is given to 100-level students and those with medical needs
• Off-campus accommodation is available in Biu town for students who don't get hostel

For enquiries, contact the **Student Affairs Division** at the university administrative block.

The NAUB Academic Calendar is published at the start of each session on the official website. For the 2024/2025 session, here is a general guide:

📅 **First Semester:**
• Resumption/Registration: October – November
• Lectures: November – February
• Semester Examinations: February – March

📅 **Second Semester:**
• Resumption/Registration: April
• Lectures: April – July
• Semester Examinations: July – August

📅 **Long Vacation:** August – September

⚠️ These are approximate dates. Always check the **official NAUB academic calendar** on www.naub.edu.ng or the notice boards on campus for the exact schedule.

Course registration at NAUB is done online through the student portal. Here's how:

1. **Log in** to the student portal at **portal.naub.edu.ng**
2. Ensure your school fees have been **fully paid** (course registration requires payment confirmation)
3. Navigate to **'Course Registration'**
4. Select your courses for the current semester
5. Submit and **print your course registration form**
6. Take the printed form to your **departmental office** for endorsement

**Important:**
• Register within the official registration period to avoid late registration fees
• A late fee of approximately ₦5,000 applies for late registration
• Maximum course load: 24 credit units per semester
• Minimum course load: 15 credit units per semester

For help, visit your Faculty Officer or the Academic Registry.

questions: how to log in to portal
answer:
The NAUB Student Portal is accessible at **portal.naub.edu.ng**.

**How to log in:**
• Username: Your **Matriculation Number** (e.g., NAUB/SCI/22/0001)
• Password: Your default password is usually your **Date of Birth** (DDMMYYYY) for first-time login

**If you forgot your password:**
1. Click **'Forgot Password'** on the login page
2. Enter your registered email address
3. A password reset link will be sent to your email
4. Alternatively, visit the **ICT Unit** on campus with your student ID for manual reset

**Available on the portal:**
• Course registration
• Fee payment (Remita)
• Hostel application
• Exam timetable
• Results (e-transcript)

For technical issues, contact the ICT Unit at **ict@naub.edu.ng**.

questions: how to change my course at NAUB
answer:
To change your course of study at NAUB, you must apply through the academic registry. Here is the procedure:

**Steps:**
1. Obtain a **Change of Course Form** from the Academic Registry
2. Fill the form and obtain the signature/approval of:
   • Your current **Head of Department (HOD)**
   • The **HOD** of the new department
   • The **Dean** of the faculty you are transferring to
3. Submit the completed form to the **Academic Registry**
4. Pay the prescribed **Change of Course fee** (approximately ₦5,000 – ₦10,000)
5. Await approval and notification via your student portal

**Eligibility:**
• Change of course is usually allowed after **100 Level**
• CGPA and O'Level requirements of the new course must be met
• Changes are typically processed at the beginning of a new session

Contact the Academic Registry for the current processing fee and timeline.

questions: how to get transcript from naub
answer:
**Result Checking:**
Results are published on the NAUB student portal (portal.naub.edu.ng) after each examination. Log in with your matric number to view your semester results.

**Result Verification/Official Transcript:**
1. Write a formal letter to the **Registrar, NAUB** requesting verification or a transcript
2. Attach a copy of your student ID and relevant results
3. Pay the prescribed fee at the Bursary (approximately ₦5,000 per copy for transcripts)
4. Submit your application to the **Academic Registry**
5. Processing takes approximately **2–4 weeks**

**Examination Remark:**
If you believe a result was wrongly graded:
1. Apply for a remark within **4 weeks** of result publication
2. Collect a **Remark Form** from the Registry
3. Pay the remark fee (approximately ₦3,000 per course)
4. Submit the form and payment receipt to the Registry

questions: how many GST courses in naub
answer:
GST (General Studies) courses are compulsory for all NAUB students regardless of faculty. They are designed to provide a broad-based education.

**Common GST Courses at NAUB:**
• GST 101 – Use of English I
• GST 102 – Use of English II
• GST 111 – Logic and Scientific Reasoning
• GST 112 – Nigerian Peoples and Culture
• GST 121 – History and Philosophy of Science
• GST 123 – Introduction to Computer Science
• GST 211 – Entrepreneurship I
• GST 212 – Entrepreneurship II
• GST 221 – Peace Studies and Conflict Resolution
• GST 222 – Introduction to Sociology

All GST courses carry 2 credit units each and are graded alongside your departmental courses.

who is in charge of naub
vice chancellor Nigerian Army University Biu

questions: how to apply for masters naub
answer:
NAUB offers postgraduate programmes through the **Postgraduate School**. Available programmes include:

**Masters Programmes (M.Sc / M.A / LLM):**
• M.Sc Computer Science
• M.Sc Mathematics
• M.A English Language
• M.Sc Economics
• LLM Law (where available)

**PhD Programmes:**
• Available in select disciplines

**Admission Requirements for Masters:**
• A good Bachelor's degree (minimum Second Class Lower) in a relevant field
• NYSC Discharge or Exemption Certificate
• At least 2 referees (academic)
• Entrance examination/interview may be required

**Application:**
Visit **www.naub.edu.ng/postgraduate** for the current application form and deadline. Contact the Postgraduate School directly for programme-specific requirements.

The NAUB University Library is located at the center of the main campus, easily accessible from all faculties.

**Library Hours:**
• Monday – Friday: 8:00 AM – 9:00 PM
• Saturday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM
• Sunday: 12:00 PM – 6:00 PM

**Services Available:**
• Physical lending of books and journals
• Digital/e-library resources
• Postgraduate research room
• Computer lab for student research
• Periodicals and newspapers

Students must present their **valid student ID** to access the library and borrow resources. The library also provides access to online databases for academic research.

questions: when is nysc for naub graduates
answer:
NYSC (National Youth Service Corps) mobilization for NAUB graduates typically occurs after the final Senate result ratification. Here's the process:

1. **Results Ratification:** Senate approves your final results and certificate
2. **Certificate Preparation:** The Academic Registry prepares your certificate and result
3. **NYSC Senate List Upload:** NAUB uploads your name to the NYSC portal (enlist.nysc.gov.ng)
4. **Personal Registration:** Log on to the NYSC portal and complete your registration
5. **Batch Assignment:** You are assigned to a deployment batch

The Academic Registry or Student Affairs Division will notify students when mobilization lists are ready. Contact **registry@naub.edu.ng** for specific enquiries.

Final year student clearance at NAUB involves visiting multiple offices to confirm no outstanding obligations. Here is the typical process:

1. **Library:** Return all borrowed books and clear any fines
2. **Bursary:** Clear all outstanding fees
3. **Hostel/Student Affairs:** Return hostel keys (if applicable)
4. **Departmental Office:** Get HOD clearance signature
5. **Faculty Office:** Get Faculty Officer signature
6. **Academic Registry:** Final clearance and collection of documents

Collect the **Clearance Form** from the Registry, complete all stages, and return the signed form. Clearance is required before your results are processed and before NYSC mobilization.

The **ICT Directorate** at NAUB is responsible for managing the university's IT infrastructure, student portal, and digital services. It is located near the Administrative Block on the main campus.

**ICT Directorate Services:**
• Student portal management and technical support
• Campus internet services
• Email and account setup for students/staff
• Maintenance of computer labs

**Contact:**
📧 ict@naub.edu.ng
🕐 Office Hours: Mon–Fri, 8:00 AM – 4:00 PM

The **Department of Computer Science** (for academic matters) is housed in the Faculty of Science building. For academic enquiries related to CS programmes, contact the HOD's office.

The **Bursary Department** handles all financial transactions at NAUB, including school fees, acceptance fees, and other levies.

**How to Pay:**
1. Log in to your student portal at **portal.naub.edu.ng**
2. Generate a **Remita Retrieval Reference (RRR)** number
3. Pay online via internet banking or at any bank branch
4. Return to the portal and confirm payment

**Bursary Office Location:** Administrative Block, NAUB main campus
**Email:** bursary@naub.edu.ng
**Hours:** Mon–Fri, 8:00 AM – 4:00 PM

Always keep your payment receipt. For any payment discrepancies, visit the Bursary with your receipt and student ID.

The **Student Union Government (SUG)** is the recognized body representing all students at NAUB. SUG elections are conducted annually by the Independent National Electoral Commission of NAUB (INEC-NAUB).

The SUG advocates for students' rights, organizes social and academic activities, and liaises between students and university management. Their office is located at the Student Centre on campus.

For concerns or issues you'd like escalated to university management, you can approach your elected SUG representatives or submit a formal complaint to the **Student Affairs Division**.

questions: what subjects for computer science naub jamb
answer:
For JAMB (UTME) to NAUB:

**General Minimum Cut-off:** 140
**Science/Computer Science/Mathematics/Physics/Chemistry:** 160+
**Law:** 180+

**Subject Combinations (examples):**
• **Computer Science:** Mathematics, Physics, Chemistry (or Biology)
• **Medicine (if available):** Biology, Chemistry, Physics
• **Law:** Literature or Government, CRS/IRS, any Social Science subject
• **Accounting/Business Admin:** Mathematics, Economics, Government/Commerce
• **English Language:** Literature, CRS/IRS, Government or History

Always confirm your exact subject combination on the **JAMB Brochure** at www.jamb.gov.ng/Brochure and verify with NAUB at www.naub.edu.ng.

questions: where is the clinic at naub
answer:
NAUB has a **Medical/Health Centre** on campus that provides basic health services to students and staff.

**Services:**
• Outpatient consultation
• First aid and emergency stabilization
• Dispensary (basic medications)
• Referral to Biu General Hospital for complex cases

**Location:** Near the Student Hostels
**Hours:** 24 hours / 7 days a week for emergencies
**Regular Clinic Hours:** 8:00 AM – 4:00 PM (Mon–Fri)

All registered students are entitled to basic health care on campus. Present your **student ID** at the clinic.
